etymology.description: 舊(舊) (jiù, "old; past") represents something that belongs to a previous time or period. 夢(夢) (mèng, "dream") refers to visions or experiences during sleep, and by extension, can refer to aspirations or past endeavors.
etymology.interpretation: 舊(舊) (jiù, "old; past") conveys the sense of something that is no longer current, tying to the previous experiences or times. 夢(夢) (mèng, "dream") connects to the experiences or aspirations that one may recall or reflect upon. Together, 舊夢 (jiùmèng) signifies a nostalgic recollection of past dreams or experiences, the old dreams that linger in memory, embodying both literal dreams and metaphorical longings or past aspirations.

examples.chinese: 有时听到老歌(有時聽到老歌)，我会想起某些旧梦(我會想起某些舊夢)。
examples.pinyin: Yǒushí tīngdào lǎo gē, wǒ huì xiǎngqǐ mǒuxiē jiùmèng.
examples.english: Sometimes when I hear old songs, I think of certain past experiences.

examples.chinese: 他的小说常常写到旧梦(他的小說常常寫到舊夢)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de xiǎoshuō chángcháng xiě dào jiùmèng.
examples.english: His novels often describe old dreams.

examples.chinese: 在那个地方(在那個地方)，他重温了一段旧梦(他重溫了一段舊夢)。
examples.pinyin: Zài nàgè dìfāng, tā chóngwēnle yī duàn jiùmèng.
examples.english: In that place, he relived a part of his past experiences.
